sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check current state after clicking purple
seg = current_frame.segmentation
print("12-pixel objects:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['pixels'] == 12:
        cr = sum(b[0] for b in n['boundary']) // len(n['boundary'])
        cc = sum(b[1] for b in n['boundary']) // len(n['boundary'])
        print(f"  ({cr},{cc}) color={n['color']}")

print("\nPurple objects:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'p' and n['pixels'] > 5:
        cr = sum(b[0] for b in n['boundary']) // len(n['boundary'])
        cc = sum(b[1] for b in n['boundary']) // len(n['boundary'])
        print(f"  ({cr},{cc}) pixels={n['pixels']}")

print("\nWhite objects:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'W' and n['pixels'] < 20:
        cr = sum(b[0] for b in n['boundary']) // len(n['boundary'])
        cc = sum(b[1] for b in n['boundary']) // len(n['boundary'])
        print(f"  ({cr},{cc}) pixels={n['pixels']}")

print("\nOrange objects:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'O' and n['pixels'] > 10:
        cr = sum(b[0] for b in n['boundary']) // len(n['boundary'])
        cc = sum(b[1] for b in n['boundary']) // len(n['boundary'])
        print(f"  ({cr},{cc}) pixels={n['pixels']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
